claim: Biogen's Phase 3 ENGAGE trial of the anti-amyloid antibody aducanumab was TERMINATED in 2019 on a pre-specified futility analysis, explicitly not for safety.
signal: Pivotal Phase 3 anti-amyloid mAb (ENGAGE) halted for futility 2019; later reversed on reanalysis and controversially approved (Aduhelm) — the canonical futility-stop that was not the program's end.
quote: Study was discontinued based on futility analysis done and not based on safety concerns.

claim: Roche TERMINATED the Phase 3 CREAD study of crenezumab in 2019 after an interim analysis indicated the anti-amyloid antibody was unlikely to meet its primary endpoint.
signal: Phase 3 anti-amyloid antibody (CREAD) terminated mid-2019 at interim for predicted endpoint failure — part of the wave of anti-amyloid Phase 3 failures before lecanemab/donanemab succeeded.
quote: This study was discontinued due to an interim analysis in the BN29552 study, which indicated that Crenezumab was unlikely to meet its primary endpoint.

claim: The Phase 3 Triumeq-in-ALS trial was STOPPED at a planned interim analysis after showing no survival benefit versus placebo on the primary outcome.
signal: Phase 3 ALS repurposing (HERV/antiretroviral hypothesis, Lighthouse) stopped at interim 2025 for no survival benefit — a clean futility termination on the primary endpoint.
quote: The results showed no benefit of Triumeq for people with ALS compared with placebo on survival, the primary outcome measure.

claim: Knopp's Phase 3 dexpramipexole extension was TERMINATED after the parent EMPOWER trial failed its primary efficacy endpoint.
signal: Phase 3 extension terminated 2013 because the parent EMPOWER trial missed its primary efficacy endpoint — a high-profile ALS asset failure that ended the program.
quote: The Initial Phase 3 Study (NCTO1281189) did not meet its primary efficacy endpoint.

claim: Biogen TERMINATED its Phase 1/2 trial of the ataxin-2 (ATXN2)-lowering antisense oligonucleotide BIIB105 in ALS by sponsor decision in 2024.
signal: A CNS antisense-against-a-genetic-modifier (ATXN2) program for ALS discontinued 2024 by sponsor decision — directly relevant to a gene-medicine pipeline's risk model.

claim: Wave Life Sciences TERMINATED its Phase 1b/2a trial of the allele-selective HTT-lowering antisense oligonucleotide WVE-120101 (PRECISION-HD1) for lack of efficacy.
signal: First-generation allele-selective HTT-lowering ASO terminated (~2021) for lack of efficacy (no significant mutant-HTT lowering) — a key negative datapoint for allele-selective ASO approaches in HD.

claim: The Phase 3 2CARE trial of coenzyme Q10 in Huntington's disease was TERMINATED after a futility analysis showed no likelihood of benefit.
signal: Phase 3 HD trial (2CARE) terminated for futility ~2014 — documents the failure of the antioxidant/mitochondrial CoQ10 hypothesis in HD. (Verbatim whyStopped retains the registry's own typos.)
quote: Futility analysis failed to showed likelihoo of benefit of CoQ 2400 mg/day.

claim: Ionis ended its MECP2 Duplication Syndrome biomarker/natural-history study early in 2025, stating the objectives were met and sufficient data collected.
target_or_drug: MECP2 (natural-history/biomarker run-in supporting an MECP2-lowering ASO program)
signal: A positive-context early termination — an MECP2-program biomarker study ended having met objectives (CSF MeCP2 biomarker), signaling an MECP2-axis gene-medicine program advancing.
quote: Sponsor decision to terminate study early due to sufficient data collected, the study objectives met and defined unmet need

claim: Encoded Therapeutics TERMINATED its ENVISION SCN1A+ Dravet syndrome natural-history study in 2023, citing a robust dataset on the syndrome's manifestations (supporting the ETX101 SCN1A gene therapy).
target_or_drug: SCN1A (ENVISION natural-history supporting ETX101 gene therapy)
signal: Positive-context termination — an SCN1A+ Dravet natural-history study ended 2023 with a robust dataset, signaling the supporting evidence base for an SCN1A-targeted gene therapy.
quote: The ENVISION study has generated a robust data set that shows consistency in the seizure and non-seizure manifestations of SCN1A+ Dravet syndrome.

claim: Takeda WITHDREW its planned Dravet/Lennox-Gastaut syndrome study in Portugal, citing a business decision.
signal: A portfolio/prioritization signal in the genetic-epilepsy space — a Dravet/LGS study withdrawn for a business decision (2024-2025).

claim: The pivotal Phase 3 ENDEAR trial of nusinersen in infantile SMA was stopped EARLY after a POSITIVE interim analysis, enabling participants to roll into an open-label study — an early termination that meant success, not failure.
signal: Landmark counter-example: the first-approved SMA therapy's pivotal trial was 'TERMINATED' early for a POSITIVE interim — proof that an early-termination status is not inherently a failure signal (read the whyStopped, not just the status).
quote: After a positive interim analysis, the decision was made to terminate the study early to allow for participants to enroll into an open label study

claim: Roche's pivotal Phase 3 GENERATION HD1 trial of the HTT-lowering antisense oligonucleotide tominersen is recorded as COMPLETED (2022); its well-known March-2021 dosing halt was a mid-trial sponsor decision NOT captured as a structured registry termination.
target_or_drug: Tominersen (RO7234292 / RG6042 / IONIS-HTTRx), non-allele-selective HTT-lowering antisense oligonucleotide
signal: Registry-vs-reality gap: status COMPLETED with NO whyStopped, although Roche halted dosing March 2021 — the halt lives in releases/literature, not the structured registry. A reminder that the registry under-captures mid-trial decisions. Roche has since advanced GENERATION HD2 (NCT05686551).
quote: A Randomized, Multicenter, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Phase III Clinical Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Intrathecally Administered RO7234292 (RG6042) in Patients With Manifest Huntington's Disease
